[2018-04-21 10:32] mqp: red with zemora isn't a bad idea. zemora is mostly just a slow value unit, if you can get it out and get enough conduits to power it without dying then it was a good thing to buy

[2018-04-21 10:37] p0lari (Polari): but if there's some decent defensive use for red (like caut here) that's moot, and if you're buying base set attackers (also like here) it should be easily worth it anyway

[2018-04-21 14:53] 307th: where are you at right now? what skill level, and what are you doing to improve?
[2018-04-21 14:53] itsotter: i played 18 games today and my mmr didn't move at all, it started at 1600 and is still there
[2018-04-21 14:54] itsotter: what i am doing: spamming ranked
[2018-04-21 14:54] itsotter: would it be more helpful to spectate top games? watch some streamer's vods maybe? i don't know if anyone is known to explain their thinking a lot
[2018-04-21 14:55] 307th: i think it's at 1700-1800 that watching top games starts being really helpful
[2018-04-21 14:55] 307th: because that's the point where set reading becomes more important
[2018-04-21 14:55] 307th: at 1600, i think most of your issues will be mechanical - stuff like, ending up with too much tech so a lot of it goes to waste
[2018-04-21 14:55] redzonex (Redzone): watching crash/msven has definitely helped me improve
[2018-04-21 14:56] 307th: or, wasting energy in the opening (wasting energy is fine in the mid to late game, but wasting energy in the opening usually puts you behind)
[2018-04-21 14:56] redzonex (Redzone): when i rejoined the game i was like 1650 and pretty easily climbed to 1800 after watching a bunch of streams and hanging out here
[2018-04-21 14:56] 307th: that could also work
[2018-04-21 14:56] 307th: it's important to watch what they're doing, not just listen to what they're saying, though
[2018-04-21 14:57] 307th: they'll be doing a lot of things automatically that you still need to learn
[2018-04-21 14:58] 307th: even if you've already read the standard style, i'd recommend re-reading it and thinking about which elements you're missing
[2018-04-21 14:58] 307th: since it isn't easy to put all of those elements into practice during a game
[2018-04-21 14:58] 307th: even if you've read about them
[2018-04-21 14:59] 307th: if you aren't sure what mistakes you're making, watching your replays can help, looking for stuff like overteching and other mistakes

[2018-04-21 15:25] Deleted User (Deleted User): Ideally you want to soak on it on the first turn it has 0 stamina
[2018-04-21 15:25] 307th: maybe 'if you'll have to buy a prompt rhino for defense next turn' is a good time to switch to rhino
[2018-04-21 15:25] 307th: hotpepper's right in theory, but in practice since tarsier is overall just a better value unit than rhino, you want to err on the side of switching late
[2018-04-21 15:26] 307th: and a lot of stuff in the random set can replace rhino, e.g. husk
[2018-04-21 15:26] 307th: if husk is in the set it's going to barely be worth it to make rhinos at all, since once you're under pressure you can just go wall + two husks + two engis
[2018-04-21 15:31] itsotter: concrete sample question: on turn 7 i did drone+engi instead of wall so i wasted a blue, but i didn't need the wall at the time
[2018-04-21 15:32] itsotter: so i had to violate "don't defend too early" or "don't waste mana" from the standard style thing
[2018-04-21 15:32] itsotter: 4FL3f-mWui1  if the situation matters
[2018-04-21 15:33] itsotter: looking at it again i guess i should have just walled
[2018-04-21 15:33] darkeisbein: in general both lines have their pros and cons, especially if there is a lot of damage incoming the following turn you might want the wall but the engi drone line gives you an extra drone which might be good
[2018-04-21 15:33] darkeisbein: also if you have to waste blue it often means you went for B to early
[2018-04-21 15:34] 1m2o: i think you went blastforge too early
[2018-04-21 15:34] itsotter: or i should have done a third play which didn't et abused by freeze
[2018-04-21 15:34] 1m2o: if i had blue which i don't need i think i would've gone spitter
[2018-04-21 15:34] 1m2o: aa i'm watching the enemy nvm
[2018-04-21 15:35] itsotter: like rhino, tarsier, engi, hold a drone, float a gold
[2018-04-21 15:35] itsotter: which is ugly but saves my wall
[2018-04-21 15:35] 307th: holding a drone would have been better, yeah
[2018-04-21 15:35] 307th: apart from that, you were fine to let the blue go to waste
[2018-04-21 15:36] 307th: like you said, sometimes you'll run into situations where you have to violate one of the principles, so in those situations don't worry too much about it
[2018-04-21 15:36] 307th: in this game i think your main mistake was not going for third engineer
[2018-04-21 15:37] 307th: energy matrix is a strong absorber, so you usually want to go 3rd engineer when it's in the random set
[2018-04-21 15:37] darkeisbein: and iceblade is really sad vs. e-matrix + walls
[2018-04-21 15:37] 307th: it's strong enough that even though you were already fully teched it still would have been worth it to add on a second blastforge just to get energy matrix
[2018-04-21 15:37] 307th: even though that would inevitably have led to letting red or blue go to waste
[2018-04-21 15:38] itsotter: i feared delaying my ability to kill iceblades if i went 3rd engi and droned up
[2018-04-21 15:38] 307th: but by going high economy you don't have to worry about that, since you'd have enough income to spend two blue and two red per turn
[2018-04-21 15:38] Deleted User (Deleted User): Nullifying 4 damage instead of 2 (3 in the case of doomed wall) is a really big deal
[2018-04-21 15:39] darkeisbein: basically energy matrix is producing 2 free engies every turn 😮
[2018-04-21 15:39] itsotter: but not if he gets a critical mass of iceblades, right?
[2018-04-21 15:39] Deleted User (Deleted User): Really its producing 4 engis every turn 😄
[2018-04-21 15:39] itsotter: and iceblades punish the doomed walls too
[2018-04-21 15:39] Deleted User (Deleted User): Getting a mass of iceblades takes a pretty long time
[2018-04-21 15:40] darkeisbein: iceblades are kinda clunky vs. energy matrix and walls
[2018-04-21 15:40] darkeisbein: since they have odd health
[2018-04-21 15:41] itsotter: alright, so go 3rd engi high econ with the big absorber
[2018-04-21 15:41] itsotter: do i then get green, set up a line of fissions, try to make it pay into trinity drones with the click for maximum greed?
[2018-04-21 15:42] itsotter: basically i do this if i think i can spend 6 per turn on the engis when the fissions are dying
[2018-04-21 15:42] 307th: fission turrets are really nice mid-game attackers, but since they have lifespan it hurts to make them as your first attackers
[2018-04-21 15:42] itsotter: but if i don't think i'll be able to, it's bad
[2018-04-21 15:42] itsotter: but i can't tell here
[2018-04-21 15:42] 307th: because they get absorbed and after a while they die
[2018-04-21 15:42] 307th: so i'd probably go animus here
[2018-04-21 15:42] 307th: one conduit wouldn't hurt since as you said you can just get a trinity drone
[2018-04-21 15:43] 307th: but my main plan would be 2 blastforges and one animus
[2018-04-21 15:43] itsotter: the super greed fission click + trinity click isn't good enough to make up for having spent time on limited-life attackers?
[2018-04-21 15:44] itsotter: i guess the money can't go anywhere in this set when it does roll in anyway
[2018-04-21 15:45] 307th: yeah, it's not a bad idea but getting value off the fission turret clicks just means the fission turrets were good
[2018-04-21 15:45] 307th: but the first few that get absorbed will still be weak, and you'll have a hard time building up enough damage to kill your opponent
[2018-04-21 15:51] darkeisbein: fission turrets as first attackers can work really well if your trying to rush

[2018-04-22 02:49] extratricky: I know I'm a bit late, but on the gauss charge vs fragile things in breachproof situations, if you're in a spot like that where both sides have gauss charges, then if you don't kill gauss charges and they kill your gauss charges with them, that results in the same situation as if you kill their gauss charges and they spend their damage on whatever else they were going to damage. So it's generally correct in those situations to kill gauss charges if the damage can't get a full kill because it just removes options.
